label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure out the order of the golfers based on the given information. There are five golfers: Amy, Eve, Mel, Mya, and Rob. The statements are: 1. Eve finished last. That means Eve is in 5th position. 2. Rob finished above Mel. So Rob is somewhere above Mel in the ranking. 3. Amy finished above Rob. So Amy is above Rob. 4. Mya finished third. So Mya is in 3rd position. Let me try to arrange them from first to fifth. We know Mya is third. So the order is: ?, ?, Mya, ?, ?. Eve is last, so fifth position is Eve. So the order is: ?, ?, Mya, ?, Eve. Now we have three positions left (1st, 2nd, and 4th) for Amy, Rob, and Mel. We know Rob finished above Mel, and Amy finished above Rob. So Amy is above Rob, who is above Mel. This means Amy must be in 1st position, Rob in 2nd position, and Mel in 4th position. Let's check if that works. Order would be: Amy, Rob, Mya, Mel, Eve. This satisfies all conditions: - Amy is above Rob. - Rob is above Mel. - Mya is third. - Eve is last. So in this order, Amy is first, Rob is second, Mya is third, Mel is fourth, and Eve is fifth. Now, let's check the options: (A) Amy finished third - No, Amy is first. (B) Eve finished third - No, Eve is fifth.
